title: lepidium densiflorum var. ramosum
intro: Lepidium densiflorum var. ramosum, also known as branched peppercress, is a plant species in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is native to the Americas. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of branched peppercress include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. It can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also sometimes used as a culinary herb, adding a peppery flavor to salads and other dishes.
